copy-move-rename.label: Copy, Move, and Rename
copy-move-rename.summary: Use cp and mv to prepare a clean handoff without losing the original when a backup matters.

sketch0.title: Use `cp` when you need a backup

sc-copy-keeps-original.title: Copy without removing original
sc-copy-keeps-original.prompt: Which command copies `a.txt` to `b.txt` and keeps `a.txt`?
sc-copy-keeps-original.hint: Read the source and destination paths from left to right before running the command.

help.concept: `cp` preserves the source by creating a copy; `mv` relocates or renames the source.
help.hint_1: Read the source and destination paths from left to right before running the command.
help.hint_2: Verify both locations: a copy leaves both files, while a move or rename removes the old path. The current task asks: Which command copies `a.txt` to `b.txt` and keeps `a.txt`?

copy-with-cp.title: Use `cp` when you need a backup
copy-with-cp.bodyMarkdown:
`cp` creates a second file while leaving the source in place. The command shape is `cp source destination`.

```bash
cp itinerary.txt backups/itinerary.txt
```

Afterward, both paths should exist. This is the right choice when you need a backup or another team needs its own copy. A reversed source and destination can overwrite the wrong file, so inspect both folders first and verify both names after the command.

move-with-mv.title: Use `mv` to move a file
move-with-mv.bodyMarkdown:
`mv` relocates a file. The source disappears from its old location and appears at the destination.

```bash
mv camera-notes.txt photo-archive/camera-notes.txt
```

The destination folder must already exist. Inspect the source and destination before moving, then list both afterward. If the old path still exists, the move did not happen as expected; if the destination name was wrong, the file may have been renamed accidentally.

rename-with-mv.title: Use `mv` to rename a file
rename-with-mv.bodyMarkdown:
`mv` also renames a file when the destination is a new name in the same folder.

```bash
mv recipe-draft.txt recipe-final.txt
```

The file content stays the same, but the old name is replaced by the new one. Choose the final name deliberately and check whether it already exists before running the command. A rename is still a move, so verify that the old path is gone and the new path is present.
